name: Railwomen: Songs and Stories of India’s Railways

location.name: Goethe-Institut / Max Mueller Bhavan New  New

keywords: Literature meets the Indian railways at the next Goethe Darbaar, as railwomen take centre stage.
description: From the rhythmic pulse of trains to the human stories carried across the country’s vast rail network, this session brings together literature and lived experience in a compelling dialogue. Acclaimed writer Rahul Bhattacharya and former railway officer Smriti Verma explore the stories, struggles, and systems that define India’s railways.Blending deeply researched literary fiction with firsthand insights from decades of frontline leadership, the conversation reflects on one of the world’s largest and most complex public institutions—not just as infrastructure, but as a living matrix of livelihoods, aspirations, and journeys.Moderated by Shrayana Bhattacharya, this session promises a rich and layered exploration of gender, labour, and storytelling within the railways.

offers.price: We have a full house; registrations are now closed.

name: Book Sale and 'Poster Women' Exhibition

location.name: Goethe-Institut / Max Mueller Bhavan New  New

description: Saturday, 18 and Sunday, 19 April 2026, 11:00 to 19:00To markWomen’s History MonthandDalit History Month,Zubaan Publishersis delighted to invite you to aMega Book Salefeaturing all available Zubaan titles—fiction, non-fiction, and academic—at discounted prices. Whether you are an avid reader or looking to gift meaningful books and merchandise to your loved ones, we have something for everyone.In addition to theBook Sale, we’re thrilled to present thePoster Women Exhibition, a curated showcase that dives deep into the visual history of the women’s movement in India. Since its inception in 2006,Poster Womenhas been a striking project that maps this incredible journey through captivating posters that tell the stories of activism, resilience, and transformation.For those seeking imaginative stories for younger readers, ourYoung Zubaancollection has you covered! We feature inspiring literature tailored for ages 4 to 16, perfect for sparking curiosity and joy in young minds.And don’t miss ourWarehouse Wonderssection, where select titles are available at a thrilling 50% discount!Plus, here’s the icing on the cake: meet some of our talented authors, engage with them, and get your books signed!Join us and spend a few hours immersed in powerful narratives that rekindle hope and celebrate feminist solidarities.Come browse, discover, and take home stories that will challenge, comfort, and inspire. We can’t wait to see you there!Panel on Saturday, 18 April 202618:00 onwardsThe Books We MadePanellists: Manisha Chaudhry, Sahba Hussain and Deepti Priya MehrotraModerator: Urvashi ButaliaConversations on Sunday, 19 April 202615:30 onwardsAnita Bharti in conversation with Shabani Hussainiwala18:00 onwardsSumitra Mehrol in conversation and Rita KothariScan the code for details:Please note:Parking is not available at the Goethe-Institut. You may find parking at the NDMC parking near the Ambadeep Building. Alternatively, you can come to the Goethe-Institut via taxi or auto-rickshaw.
